name: Shuster Law, PLLC
description_short: Bankruptcy law firm in Lewisville, TX offering Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filing assistance, debt settlement, and personal injury representation with 20+ years of experience.
description_long:
Shuster Law, PLLC is a Texas-based law firm established in 2002, headquartered in Lewisville with additional offices in Dallas, Fort Worth, Arlington, Denton, and Flower Mound. Attorney David Shuster and his staff serve the Dallas-Fort Worth region and surrounding areas, claiming to have assisted thousands of clients with millions of dollars in debt discharge over two decades. The firm operates with extended service options including virtual consultations for clients unable to attend in-person meetings.

The firm's core service is bankruptcy representation, specifically helping clients determine whether Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 filing is appropriate for their situation. Beyond bankruptcy, Shuster Law offers debt settlement services for clients seeking to resolve debts without filing, and personal injury representation covering the full process from medical treatment coordination to settlement negotiation. The firm also handles debt collection defense and civil litigation matters.

Key distinguishing factors include: membership in the Bankruptcy Section of the Tarrant County Bar Association, bilingual services (Spanish-language support advertised), free initial consultations, payment plans for legal fees, and virtual meeting options. The firm explicitly positions itself on experience and pre-litigation settlement capabilities, emphasizing that many cases can be resolved before formal court filing to save clients time and legal costs.

A significant limitation is the firm's focus on bankruptcy and debt matters in Texas only—their service area is geographically limited. Additionally, while the website claims "thousands of clients" and "millions of debt discharged," specific case outcomes, success rates, or client testimonials are absent. The firm is a solo/small practice, not a large bankruptcy mill, which may mean limited availability depending on caseload.
address: 1100 Northwest Loop 410 Suite 700, San Antonio, TX 78213

pros: 20+ years of combined legal experience specifically in bankruptcy, debt settlement, and personal injury law
Membership in Bankruptcy Section of Tarrant County Bar Association provides professional credibility
Free initial consultations with no obligation to hire
Payment plans available to make legal representation more accessible
Multiple office locations across DFW (Lewisville, Dallas, Fort Worth, Arlington, Denton, Flower Mound)
Virtual consultation options for clients who cannot attend in-person meetings
cons: No client testimonials, case results, or success rates published on website to verify claimed outcomes
Service limited to Texas only; cannot assist consumers outside state
Small firm size may limit availability for immediate consultations during busy periods
Website lacks specific pricing information or transparent fee structure for different bankruptcy chapters
Claims of 'thousands of clients' and 'millions of debt discharged' are unverified and not substantiated with case data
best_for: Texas residents in DFW area facing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy decisions
Individuals seeking debt settlement alternatives before filing bankruptcy
Spanish-speaking consumers needing bilingual legal representation
People injured in accidents seeking personal injury representation and settlement
diagnosis: Shuster Law is best for Texas residents in the Dallas-Fort Worth area who need guidance on bankruptcy filing options or debt resolution without bankruptcy. The main caveat is that you cannot verify their claimed success rates or client outcomes from the website, and their services are limited to Texas jurisdiction only.
services: Medical treatment coordination for injury cases
Civil litigation in state and federal courts
